taxonomy_v3: Engineering and Development, Software Engineering, Backend Software Engineer
core_skills: TypeScript, Backend services, Cloud environment, Event-driven architecture, Messaging systems
skill_match_scores: TypeScript:1.0, Backend services:1.0, Cloud environment:1.0, Event-driven architecture:0.0, Messaging systems:0.0
must_have_quals: 2–4 years of software engineering experience, with meaningful exposure to backend services in a cloud environment, Comfort with TypeScript or a strong willingness to go deep on it quickly: this is our primary language, Familiarity with event-driven architecture and messaging systems; Kafka or similar experience is a plus, Infrastructure fluency: you understand what your code runs on and can write or modify Terraform when needed, Security-conscious by default: you think about auth, least-privilege access, and data sensitivity without being prompted, AI-forward mindset: you use AI tooling in your development workflow and think actively about where agentic approaches can replace manual work, Strong written and verbal communication: this squad works across team boundaries constantly
core_responsibilities: TypeScript Lambda microservices and API Gateway integrations across our AWS-native stack, Event-driven workflows using Apache Kafka (via Redpanda Managed) for asynchronous, decoupled system communication, Infrastructure as code with Terraform and Spacelift, owning deployments from first commit to production, Resilient service patterns: retry logic, dead-letter queues, graceful degradation, idempotent handlers: that make the platform boring in the best way, Agentic AI solutions using Claude Code and the Anthropic API, including automated workflows and intelligent processing pipelines, Shared libraries, tooling, and internal standards that raise the quality floor for the entire engineering organization
job_summary: Jewelers Mutual Group is a technology-focused organization committed to supporting the Jewelry industry since 1913. They are seeking a Software Engineer to work on the Core Services squad, which owns the foundational platform for their engineering organization, focusing on expanding the platform with AI-assisted development and resilient service patterns.
